tool.name: terminal_execute
tool.description: Execute a bash command in a persistent terminal session. The terminal maintains state (environment variables, current directory, running processes) between commands.

parameter.name: command
parameter.description: The bash command to execute. Can be empty to check output of running commands (waits for timeout period to collect output).

parameter.name: is_input
parameter.description: If true, the command is sent as input to a currently running process. If false (default), the command is executed as a new bash command.

parameter.name: timeout
parameter.description: Optional timeout in seconds for command execution. CAPPED AT 60 SECONDS. If not provided, uses default wait (30s). On timeout, the command keeps running and returns status 'running'.

parameter.name: terminal_id
parameter.description: Identifier for the terminal session. Defaults to "default". Use different IDs to manage multiple concurrent terminal sessions.

parameter.name: no_enter
parameter.description: If true, don't automatically add Enter/newline after the command. Useful for interactive prompts, Vim navigation (e.g., "gg", "5j", "i"), or multi-step commands.

returns.description: Response containing: content (command output), exit_code (only for completed), command, terminal_id, status ('completed' or 'running'), working_dir.

tool.examples:
# Execute a simple command
ls -la
# Run a command with timeout
npm install 60
# Background process
python app.py > server.log 2>&1 &
# Interrupt (C-c works automatically)
C-c
# Interactive input
y true
